title: Upgrading kubeadm clusters — Drain the node
text:
Prepare the node for maintenance by marking it unschedulable and evicting the workloads:
# replace <node-to-drain> with the name of your node you are draining
kubectl drain <node-to-drain> --ignore-daemonsets

On Linux nodes, the kubelet defaults to supporting only cgroups v2.
For Kubernetes 1.36 the
FailCgroupV1
kubelet configuration option is set to
true
by default.
To learn more, refer to the
Kubernetes cgroup v1 deprecation documentation
.
Upgrade the kubelet and kubectl:
Ubuntu, Debian or HypriotOS
CentOS, RHEL or Fedora
# replace x in 1.36.x-* with the latest patch version
sudo apt-mark unhold kubelet kubectl
&&
\
sudo apt-get update
&&
sudo apt-get install -y
kubelet
=
'1.36.x-*'
kubectl
=
'1.36.x-*'
&&
\
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubectl
For systems with DNF:
# replace x in 1.36.x-* with the latest patch version
sudo yum install -y kubelet-
'1.36.x-*'
kubectl-
'1.36.x-*'
--disableexcludes
=
kubernetes
For systems with DNF5:
# replace x in 1.36.x-* with the latest patch version
sudo yum install -y kubelet-
'1.36.x-*'
kubectl-
'1.36.x-*'
--setopt
=
disable_excludes
=
kubernetes
Restart the kubelet:
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart kubelet
